mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positively correlated with 20-day SPX forward returns (Rank IC > 0) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor income expectations and corporate revenue risk, generating negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE has risen > 0.3pp over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) jointly indicate a contractionary demand-supply coordination failure that amplifies downside equity risk beyond either signal alone, predicting negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO do not produce significantly negative 20-day SPX returns compared to instances with only one declining (difference in means t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, month-over-month increases in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal accelerating cost pressures that the Fed must counteract, producing negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns as real rates rise; this mechanism is inactive outside tightening cycles.
failure_condition: During months where FEDFUNDS has increased over the prior 3 months, positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.5 index points signals a contraction in real output that precedes downward earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines > 0.5 points are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising unemployment (UNRATE) and falling payroll employment (PAYEMS) create a labor market deterioration signal stronger than either series alone, predicting negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns as consumption expectations collapse.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS does not underperform the independent UNRATE-only signal by at least 0.05 in 60-day forward return Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: During housing-market-led expansions, month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal durable demand strength and construction sector earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns; this relationship weakens when housing is not the cycle driver.
failure_condition: During periods classified as housing-led expansion, month-over-month HOUST increases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a stagnant or falling federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a negative real rate shock that compresses real yields and drives capital into equities, predicting positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: The interaction of rising CPIAUCSL and flat-or-falling FEDFUNDS does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
